introSection: Motocoaster was an Intamin launched motorbike roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened on 28 September 2007 and was associated with Australian motorcyclist Mick Doohan up until 2021.

On 15 January 2026, the park posted on Facebook that the ride would permanently close on 1 February 2026.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content:
The last two cars in each train had a regular motorbike seat and a sidecar seat, which had a lower height limit.

The design of the seats and restraints were a complex prototype by Intamin which prioritised authenticity to the motorbike design. Subsequent rides of this model have used simpler restraint systems.

fullText:
Motocoaster was an Intamin launched motorbike roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia.

The ride originally opened on 28 September 2007 and was associated with Australian motorcyclist Mick Doohan up until 2021.

On 15 January 2026, the park posted on Facebook that the ride would permanently close on 1 February 2026.



The last two cars in each train had a regular motorbike seat and a sidecar seat, which had a lower height limit.

The design of the seats and restraints were a complex prototype by Intamin which prioritised authenticity to the motorbike design. Subsequent rides of this model have used simpler restraint systems.
